questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the best connective: '____ the equipment was old, it functioned reliably.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A contrast between two clauses is introduced by a concessive conjunction.

**Working.** Old equipment working reliably is unexpected, so a contrast word ('Although') is needed; 'Because' would imply causation.

**Answer.** Although.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The perimeter of a square of side $9$ is _____ .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A can finish a job in $12$ days and B in $6$ days. Working together, they finish it in _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the per-day work rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{1}{6}=\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{2}{12}=\dfrac{3}{12}=\dfrac{1}{4}$, so $4$ days.

**Answer.** $4$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the average of the four bar values shown: $4, 6, 8, 10$.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='90' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='85' y='75' width='25' height='45'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='45' width='25' height='75'/></g><text x='55' y='138' font-size='8'>4, 6, 8, 10</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A wall clock seen in a mirror appears to read $4{:}00$. The actual time is

figure.caption: A clock face as seen in a mirror, appearing to read 4 o'clock.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='70' cy='70' r='55' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='70' y1='70' x2='70' y2='30' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='70' y1='70' x2='105' y2='90' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='3'/><text x='62' y='20' font-size='11' fill='#64748b'>12</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Actual time $=12{:}00$ minus the mirror reading.

**Working.** $12{:}00-4{:}00=8{:}00$.

**Answer.** $8{:}00$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A bag contains $3$ red and $2$ blue balls. The probability of drawing a red ball at random is

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A number increased by $20\%$ becomes $90$. The original number is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Increasing by $20\%$ multiplies the number by $1.2$.

**Working.**
$$x\times 1.2 = 90 \Rightarrow x = \frac{90}{1.2} = 75$$

**Answer.** $75$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Read the passage and answer.

'Acid mine drainage occurs when sulphide minerals react with water and oxygen. The resulting acidic water can dissolve heavy metals, contaminating nearby streams long after a mine has closed.'

According to the passage, acid mine drainage:
options: can continue to pollute water even after mining stops
occurs only while a mine is operating
is caused by the complete absence of oxygen
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Match the option to the explicit wording of the passage.

**Working.** The text states contamination persists 'long after a mine has closed', so pollution can continue post-closure. Oxygen is required (not absent), and metals are dissolved (not neutralised).

**Answer.** can continue to pollute water even after mining stops.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On a standard die, opposite faces sum to $7$. If the top face shows $2$, the bottom face shows

figure.caption: A die with two pips showing on its top face.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='25' y='25' width='70' height='70' rx='8' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='45' cy='45' r='6' fill='#334155'/><circle cx='75' cy='75' r='6' fill='#334155'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite faces of a standard die add to $7$.

**Working.** $7-2=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.
